title: Database pool exhaustion after a max_connections change
symptom: web tier latency breached the 100ms SLA
resolution: Rolled back the config change that lowered max_connections from 500 to 150; connections drained and latency recovered within one cycle.
summary: A configuration push reduced the database connection ceiling, connections pinned flat at the new limit, and the application and web tiers queued behind it. Rolling the change back restored service.

title: Application capacity exhaustion cascading to the web tier
symptom: web tier response times degraded sharply
resolution: Scaled the application worker pool and fixed the slow downstream call that was blocking workers; queue drained and latency normalised.
summary: The application tier saturated its worker pool behind a slow dependency, its request queue grew while throughput stayed flat, and the web tier latency rose in step. Fixing the downstream block resolved it.

title: Volumetric DDoS flood at the edge load balancer
symptom: load balancer connection count spiked and legitimate requests failed
resolution: Enabled rate limiting and SYN-cookie protection, blocked the abusive source ranges, and engaged upstream scrubbing.
summary: A sudden flood of new connections from a narrow set of sources exhausted socket capacity at the edge while backend CPU stayed low. Edge rate limiting and scrubbing absorbed the burst.

title: NIC failure dropping frames on a switch port
symptom: packet loss and error rate rose on a single host path
resolution: Moved the workload to the standby bond member, replaced the failing NIC and cable, and cleared counters.
summary: Interface error and discard counters climbed on one host while others stayed clean, isolating a hardware NIC fault. Failing over to the standby interface and replacing the NIC cleared the loss.

title: Link degradation on a core transport span
symptom: packet loss and jitter increased across a network path
resolution: Failed traffic to the redundant path, replaced a degraded optic, and opened a carrier ticket for the provider span.
summary: CRC and error counters on a transport interface grew with rising loss and jitter, pointing at a degrading optical span. Draining the link and replacing the optic restored the path.

title: Port-scan reconnaissance sweep against the edge
symptom: load balancer saw a burst of short-lived connections to many ports
resolution: Blocked the scanning source at the edge ACL, confirmed no unintended service exposure, and raised a security ticket.
summary: Many short connections to distinct ports from a single source, correlated with IDS alerts, identified a scan rather than real load. Blocking the source at the edge stopped the noise.

title: Bad DNS record push breaking name resolution
symptom: application error rate spiked on failed name lookups
resolution: Rolled back the erroneous DNS record, flushed caches, and lowered the TTL to speed propagation.
summary: A zone edit introduced a wrong record just before onset, producing SERVFAIL/NXDOMAIN responses that drove application errors. Reverting the record and flushing caches recovered resolution.

title: Bad configuration push regressing the web tier
symptom: web tier latency and errors rose immediately after a deploy
resolution: Rolled the deploy back to the last-known-good configuration; the symptom cleared within one propagation cycle.
summary: A deployment changed an application parameter seconds before onset and the web tier degraded in step. Rolling back to the previous configuration restored normal behaviour.
